strengths: Demonstrated good traction control with the Maryland grasper throughout the cutting sequence
Maintained steady scissor positioning and controlled cutting motion
Successfully followed the circular path for most of the circumference
weaknesses: Failed to complete the circle cut, leaving material bridges that prevented full separation
Did not verify complete circle separation before task completion
May have rushed the final cutting phase without ensuring thorough completion

drills.name: Circle tracking quarter-cuts
drills.description: Cut the circle in four controlled quadrants while maintaining traction, pausing between each quarter to verify complete separation before proceeding.
drills.targets_weakness: Failed to complete the circle cut.

drills.name: Completion verification drill
drills.description: After each practice circle cut, use the Maryland grasper to gently lift and test that the inner circle is completely free from the outer material.
drills.targets_weakness: Did not verify complete circle separation.

progression_note: At the intermediate level, focus on converting your good cutting technique into complete task execution by developing systematic verification habits before declaring completion.
